key_insight: PF-DIC integrates phase-field fracture concepts into digital image correlation to measure displacement, strain, and damage in a unified, crack-aware framework.
engineering_relevance: It enables more accurate full-field measurements near cracks and reduces manual masking effort, making it attractive for fracture testing, defect characterization, and structural health monitoring.
method: Use a phase-field variable to represent complex crack growth and morphology directly from speckle images.
Embed damage into the DIC optimization so displacement and strain are solved with crack-aware constraints.
Implement the framework with a finite element formulation for experimental-image-based field recovery.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical examples showing that PF-DIC captures different crack types while improving displacement accuracy, especially near crack tips.
watch_item: The method adds modeling and implementation complexity, so performance may depend on robust parameter selection and reliable phase-field calibration for real experiments.
paper_title: PF-DIC: Phase field digital image correlation for integrated full-field displacement, strain, and damage measurements
